product_overview: NexusChat is a lightweight, self-hosted internal real-time chat platform with role-based permissions (admin/moderator/participant), room management, and slash-commands. It provides instant messaging, smooth-scroll UX, and server-enforced access control via Supabase RLS.
core_goals: Provide real-time messaging with sub-500ms visible delivery across connected clients
Enforce role-based permissions server-side (RLS) so UI restrictions cannot be bypassed
Enable admin room management (create/delete) and role management in a demo-ready MVP
Deliver a polished, dark-themed UX with smooth scrolling (Lenis) and subtle animations (anime.js)
Deploy a live URL and ensure the app works offline-to-online with clear reconnect/empty states

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: User registration, login, session persistence, and fetching the current user profile.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/register with {email,password} -> Receive 201 Created with user id -> POST /api/auth/login with same credentials -> Receive 200 with JWT token -> GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer {token} -> Receive 200 with user profile (role=participant)

POST /api/auth/register with an existing email -> Receive 409 Conflict with message 'Account exists' -> POST /api/auth/login with wrong password -> Receive 401 Unauthorized with message 'Invalid credentials' -> GET /api/auth/me without token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Room Management API
features.description: List rooms for authenticated users; create and delete rooms (admin-only). New rooms are propagated in real-time to connected clients.
user_flows: GET /api/rooms with Authorization: Bearer {token} -> Receive 200 with ordered rooms list -> POST /api/rooms with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} and body {name:'engineering'} -> Receive 201 Created with room id -> GET /api/rooms with Authorization: Bearer {token} -> Receive 200 with rooms list including the new room (realtime subscribers also receive update)

POST /api/rooms with Authorization: Bearer {participant-token} and body {name:'general'} -> Receive 403 Forbidden (RLS blocks insert) -> POST /api/rooms with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} and body {name:''} -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error 'name required'

DELETE /api/rooms/{room_id} with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} -> Receive 200 OK (cascade deletes messages) -> GET /api/rooms with token -> Receive 200 without deleted room -> Connected clients in deleted room receive redirect/empty-state event
DELETE /api/rooms/{room_id} with Authorization: Bearer {participant-token} -> Receive 403 Forbidden (RLS prevents delete)

features.name: Messaging API & Realtime
features.description: Fetch message history for a room and send/receive live messages. Realtime broadcasts INSERT/DELETE events so clients stay in sync.
user_flows: GET /api/rooms/{room_id}/messages with Authorization: Bearer {token} -> Receive 200 with ordered message history (id, content, created_at, sender_id, profiles(display_name,role))

Supabase client: supabase.from('messages').insert({room_id, sender_id, content}) with valid session -> Receive 201 Created -> Realtime channel room:{room_id} broadcasts INSERT -> Connected clients receive INSERT event and fetch/merge sender profile -> New message renders with anime.js animation
GET /api/rooms/{room_id}/messages with invalid/expired token -> Receive 401 Unauthorized -> Client shows 'Session expired. Please log in again.' toast and redirects to /login

Supabase client: supabase.from('messages').insert({room_id, sender_id:other_user_id, content}) where auth.uid() != sender_id -> Receive 403 / RLS error (permission denied) -> Client shows toast 'You don't have permission to do this.'

features.name: Role Management (Profiles)
features.description: Admins can view users and update roles. Role changes are enforced by RLS and are broadcast to clients via realtime profile updates.
user_flows: GET /api/auth/me with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} -> Receive 200 with admin profile -> GET /api/profiles with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} -> Receive 200 with users list -> PATCH /api/profiles/{targetUserId} with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} and body {role:'moderator'} -> Receive 200 OK -> Realtime profiles channel broadcasts role change -> Clients update role badge and UI permissions

PATCH /api/profiles/{targetUserId} with Authorization: Bearer {participant-token} and body {role:'moderator'} -> Receive 403 Forbidden (RLS prevents non-admin role updates) -> Client shows toast 'You don't have permission to do this.'

PATCH /api/profiles/{targetUserId} with Authorization: Bearer {admin-token} and body {role:'invalid_role'} -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error 'role must be one of admin, moderator, participant'

features.name: Slash Commands & System Messages
features.description: Client-side slash command parsing for quick helpers (/help, /rooms) and server-side command actions for privileged operations (/kick, /promote). System command outputs are presented as styled system messages (not stored as regular messages unless appropriate).
user_flows: Client: detect input '/help' -> Local command parser handles '/help' -> Returns system message payload -> UI renders system message (no DB write) visible to the issuing user or room subscribers
Client: detect input '/rooms' -> Local command parser handles '/rooms' using current rooms cache -> Returns system message with room list -> UI renders system message

Client: POST /api/rooms/{room_id}/commands with Authorization: Bearer {mod-token} and body {command:'/kick', target:'@username'} -> Server (or Supabase RPC) executes kick -> Receive 200 OK -> Realtime room channel broadcasts system event and affected user is removed from room

Client: POST /api/rooms/{room_id}/commands with Authorization: Bearer {participant-token} and body {command:'/promote', target:'@username'} -> Receive 403 Forbidden -> Client displays system-style error message 'You don't have permission to use this command.'

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: User registration, login, and profile management
